ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Na Onyankopɔn frɛɛ ewiem no ɔsoro. Na adeɛ sae, na adeɛ kyee, da a ɛtɔ so mmienu ne no.
translated: And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Onyankopɔn bɔɔ onipa wɔ ne suban so. Aane, Nyankopɔn suban so na ɔbɔɔ no, ɔbarima ne ɔbaa na ɔbɔɔ wɔn.
translated: And God created man in His own image. Yes, in the image of God He created him; male and female He created them.

ref: I have provided all kinds of fruit and grain for you to eat.
text: Na Onyankopɔn kaa sɛ: Hwɛ, mede mfudeɛ a ɛwɔ asase ani nyinaa ne nnuaba nyinaa mama mo sɛ ɛnyɛ mo aduane.
translated: And God said: Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in which is the fruit of a tree yielding seed; to you it shall be for food.

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Na wɔwiee ɔsoro ne asase ne deɛ ɛwɔ mu nyinaa bɔ.
translated: And they finished creating the heavens and the earth and all that is in them.

ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: Na afuo mu nnua nyinaa nnya mmaa asase so, na afuo mu nhahan nyinaa nnya mfifirii, ɛfiri sɛ na AWURADE Nyankopɔn nnya ntɔɔ nsuo nguu asase no so, na na onipa a ɔbɛdɔ asase nni hɔ.
translated: And no shrub of the field had yet appeared on the earth and no plant of the field had yet sprung up, for the LORD God had not sent rain on the earth and there was no man to work the ground.

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: Na mmom papa ne bɔne ho nimdeɛ dua no deɛ, nni; ɛfiri sɛ da a wobɛdi bie no, wuo na wobɛwuo.
translated: But as for the knowledge of good and evil, do not eat of it; because the day you eat of it, you will surely die.

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,
text: Na AWURADE Nyankopɔn maa nnahɔɔ tɔɔ onipa no so, na ɔdae. Na ɔyii ne mfe mpadeɛ mu baako, na ɔde nam tuaa ananmu.
translated: And the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and he slept. And He took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh instead thereof.

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Na AWURADE Nyankopɔn yɛɛ nwoma ntadeɛ maa Adam ne ne yere de hyɛɛ wɔn. Wɔpamo Adam ne Hawa firi turo no mu
translated: And the LORD God made garments of clothing for Adam and his wife and clothed them. They drove Adam and Eve out of the garden.

ref: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?
text: Na AWURADE bisaa Kain sɛ: Adɛn nti na wo bo afuo na woamuna yi?
translated: And the LORD asked Cain: Why are you angry and why has your face fallen?

ref: If you had done the right thing, you would be smiling. But you did the wrong thing, and now sin is waiting to attack you like a lion. Sin wants to destroy you, but don't let it!
text: Sɛ woyɛɛ no yie a, anka w’anim rente anaa? Na sɛ woanyɛ no yie enti, bɔne butu ɛpono ano, na w’akyiri kwan na ɛhwehwɛ, nanso di ne so.
translated: If you had done it well, wouldn’t you have been accepted? But because you didn’t do it well, sin is crouching at the door, and it desires you, but you must rule over it.

ref: But Cain had to go far from the Lord and live in the Land of Wandering, which is east of Eden. More and More People
text: Na Kain firii AWURADE anim kɔe, na ɔkɔtenaa Nod asase so wɔ Eden apueeɛ. Kain asefoɔ
translated: So Cain went out from the presence of the LORD and lived in the land of Nod, east of Eden. The descendants of Cain

ref: Anyone who tries to get even with me will be punished ten times more than anyone who tries to get even with Cain.”
text: Na sɛ wɔtɔɔ Kain so werɛ mprɛnson a, Lamek deɛ bɛyɛ aduɔson nson. Set ne Enos awoɔ
translated: And if Cain’s vengeance is sevenfold, then Lamech’s will be seventy-sevenfold. Seth and Enosh were born.

ref: Adam and his wife had another son. They named him Seth, because they said, “God has given us a son to take the place of Abel, who was killed by his brother Cain.”
text: Na Adam ne ne yere daeɛ bio, na ɔwoo ɔbabarima, na ɔtoo ne din Set sɛ: Onyankopɔn de ɔba foforɔ asi me Habel a Kain kumm no no ananmu.
translated: Adam slept with his wife again, and she gave birth to a son and named him Seth, saying: God has given me another child in place of Abel, whom Cain killed.

ref: More and more people were born, until finally they spread all over the earth. Some of their daughters were so beautiful that supernatural beings came down and married the ones they wanted.
text: Nyankopɔn mma hunuu nnipa mmammaa sɛ wɔn ho yɛ fɛ, na wɔwarewaree wɔn mu biara a wɔpɛ.
translated: God’s children saw that humans were beautiful, and they married any of them they wanted.

ref: He was sorry that he had made them,
text: Na AWURADE nuu ne ho sɛ ɔyɛɛ onipa wɔ asase soɔ, na ɛyɛɛ no ya n’akomam.
translated: And the LORD regretted that he had made man on the earth, and it grieved him at his heart.

ref: and he said, “I'm going to destroy every person on earth! I'll even wipe out animals, birds, and reptiles. I'm sorry I ever made them.”
text: Na AWURADE kaa sɛ: Mɛpepa nnipa a mabɔ wɔn no afiri asase ani, ampa ara, ɛfiri onipa so kɔsi ntɔteboa ne mmoa a wɔwea ne ewiem nnomaa so, ɛfiri sɛ manu me ho sɛ meyɛɛ wɔn.
translated: And the LORD said: I will wipe mankind, whom I have created, from the face of the earth—both people and animals, creatures that move along the ground and birds of the air—for I regret that I have made them.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Na wo deɛ, boaboa aduane nyinaa bi ano kɔ mu, na ayɛ wo ne wɔn aduane.
translated: But as for you, you gather all kinds of food together and take it inside, and it becomes food for you and them.

ref: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
text: Na nsuo no yirii wɔ asase so nna ɔha aduonum.
translated: And the water dried upon the earth after one hundred and fifty days.

ref: After Noah and his family had left the boat,
text: Na Noa ne ne yere, ne ne mma ne wɔn yerenom firii adi;
translated: Then Noah and his wife, his sons and their wives came out.

ref: When I see the rainbow in the sky, I will always remember the promise that I have made to every living creature.
text: Na nyankontɔn no bɛba mununkum mu, na mahwɛ, na mede makae daa apam a ɛwɔ Onyankopɔn ne nnipa ne abɔdeɛ mu ateasefoɔ a ɛwɔ asase soɔ nyinaa no ntam.
translated: And the rainbow will appear in the clouds, and I will look at it and remember the everlasting covenant between God and humans and all living creatures of every kind on the earth.

ref: One day he got drunk and was lying naked in his tent.
text: Na ɔnom nsa no bi, na ɔboroe, na ɔda ne ntomadan mu a ne ho da hɔ.
translated: And he drank some alcohol, and he became drunk, and he lay uncovered inside his tent.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Na Noa ani so tete firii ne nsaborɔ mu na ɔtee deɛ ne ba kumaa no ayɛ no no,
translated: Noah had been worried for a long time about how his youngest child was behaving towards him,

ref: When the Egyptians see you, they will murder me because I am your husband. But they won't kill you.
text: na sɛ Misraimfoɔ hu wo a, wɔbɛka sɛ: Ne yere nie, na wɔaku me, na wɔama woatena ase.
translated: And if the Egyptians see you, they will say: This is his wife, and they will kill me, but let you live.

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Adɛn nti na wose: Ɔyɛ me nua? Anka meremfa no nware no; na afei, hwɛ wo yere nie, fa no na kɔ wo kwan.
translated: Why then do you say: He is my brother? Otherwise, I wouldn’t have let him sleep with me; and now, look, here is your wife, take her and go your way.

ref: Abram stayed in the land of Canaan. But Lot settled near the cities of the valley and put up his tents not far from Sodom,
text: Abram tenaa Kanaan asase so, na Lot deɛ, ɔtenaa Yordan kanko no nkuro mu, na ɔtutuu ne ntomadan ara de kɔduruu Sodom.
translated: Abram lived in the land of Canaan, but Lot lived among the cities of the plain of the Jordan, and pitched his tents near Sodom.

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Na Abram tutuu ne ntomadan, na ɔbɛtenaa Mamre pɔ a ɛwɔ Hebron no ho, na ɔsii afɔrebukyia maa AWURADE wɔ hɔ.
translated: Then Abram moved his tent and went to live near the great trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar to the LORD.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Na afe a ɛtɔ so dunan mu no, Kedorlaomer ne ahemfo a wɔkeka ne ho no bɛkum Refaim wɔ Asterot-karnaim, ne Susifoɔ wɔ Ham, ne Emifoɔ Kiriataim mpaperɛ so,
translated: And in the fourteenth year, Kedorlaomer and the kings who were with him defeated the Rephaim in Ashteroth Karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, and the Emim in the plain of Kiriathaim,

ref: They went back to the city of Enmishpat, better known as Kadesh. Then they captured all the land that belonged to the Amalekites, and they defeated the Amorites who were living in Hazazon-Tamar.
text: Na wɔsane baa Enmispat, , na wɔkaa Amalekfoɔ asase nyinaa ne Amorifoɔ nso a wɔte Haseson-tamar no gui.
translated: Then they returned to Enmishpat, and they destroyed all the land of the Amalekites and also the Amorites who lived in Hazezon-tamar.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Na wɔfaa Sodom ne Gomora nnoɔma ne wɔn nnuane nyinaa de sii mu kɔe.
translated: And they took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah and all their food and went away.

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek
text: Na ɔde nnoɔma no nyinaa ne ne nua ba Lot ne ne nnoɔma ne mmaa no nso ne nkurɔfoɔ no sane bae. Melkisedek hyira Abram
translated: And he brought back all the possessions, and also brought back his nephew Lot and his possessions, as well as the women and the people. Melchizedek blessed Abram.

ref: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “All I want are my people. You can keep everything else.”
text: Na Sodom hene ka kyerɛɛ Abram sɛ: Fa nnipa no ma me, na fa ahodeɛ no.
translated: Then the king of Sodom said to Abram: Give me the people, and take the possessions for yourself.

ref: Then the Lord told him, “Bring me a three-year-old cow, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.”
text: Na AWURADE ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ: Fa nantwie ba a wadi mfeɛ mmiɛnsa, abirekyie a wadi mfeɛ mmiɛnsa, odwennini a wadi mfeɛ mmiɛnsa, anyankuku ne aborɔnoma ba brɛ me.
translated: And the LORD said to him: Bring a three-year-old heifer, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon to me.

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Na ɔfrɛɛ AWURADE a ɔne no kasaeɛ no din sɛ: Wone El-roi, na ɔse: Mahunu deɛ ɔhu me no ampa anaa?
translated: And she called the name of the LORD who spoke to her: You are El-roi, for she said, Have I truly seen the One who sees me?

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Na Onyankopɔn ka kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ: Wo yere Sarai deɛ, mfrɛ no Sarai bio, na mmom Sara.
translated: And God said to Abraham: As for your wife Sarai, do not call her Sarai anymore, but rather Sarah.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Na Onyankopɔn kaa sɛ: Ampaneampa, wo yere Sara bɛwo ɔba ama wo, na woato ne din Isak. Na mɛma me ne no apam no agyina ayɛ daa apam ama n’asefoɔ a ɛbɛdi n’akyire no.
translated: And God said: Truly, your wife Sarah will bear a son for you, and you shall name him Isaac. And I will establish my covenant with him as an everlasting covenant for his descendants after him.

ref: He then served his guests some yogurt and milk together with the meat. While they were eating, he stood near them under the tree,
text: Na Abraham faa nufosuo ani sradeɛ ne nufosuo ne nantwie ba nam a ɔsiesiee no no, na ɔde bɛsii wɔn anim; na ɔno deɛ, ɔgyinaa wɔn nkyɛn dua no ase, maa wɔdidii.
translated: And Abraham took butter and milk and the calf which he had prepared, and set it before them; and he stood by them under the tree, and they ate.

ref: The Lord asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh? Does she doubt that she can have a child in her old age?
text: Na AWURADE bisaa Abraham sɛ: Adɛn nti na Sara seree sɛ: Ampaneampa ara manyini yi mɛwo ɔba?
translated: And the LORD asked Abraham: Why did Sarah laugh, saying: Truly, shall I bear a child now that I am old?

ref: Let's get our father drunk! Then we can sleep with him and have children.”
text: Bra, na yɛmma yɛn agya nsa nnom, na yɛne no nkɔda, na yɛanya yɛn agya ho mma.
translated: Come, let us give our father drink, and let us lie with him, that we may have children by our father.

ref: That's how Lot's two daughters had children.
text: Na Lot mma maa baanu no nyinaa nyinsɛn wɔn agya ho.
translated: So both of Lot's daughters became pregnant by their father.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Na Onyankopɔn baa Abimelek nkyɛn anadwo daeɛ mu, na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ: Hwɛ, ɔbaa a woakɔfa no no nti, woawu awie, ɛfiri sɛ ɔyɛ ɔbarima bi yere.
translated: But God came to Abimelech in a dream at night and said to him: Look, because of the woman you have taken, you are as good as dead, for she is another man's wife.

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
text: Na Abimelek kaa sɛ: Hwɛ, m’asase na ɛda w’anim yi, baabiara a ɛyɛ wɔ w’ani soɔ no, tena.
translated: And Abimelech said: Look, my land is before you; settle wherever it pleases you.

ref: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.
text: Na Abraham ba Isak dii nnawɔtwe no, ɔtwaa no twetia sɛdeɛ Onyankopɔn hyɛɛ no no.
translated: When Abraham's son Isaac was eight days old, he circumcised him as God had commanded him.

ref: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away
text: Na abɔfra no nyinii, na wɔtwaa no nufoɔ. Na da a wɔtwaa Isak nufoɔ no, Abraham too pono kɛseɛ. Wɔpamo Hagar ne Ismael
translated: And the child grew, and was weaned. And on the day Isaac was weaned, Abraham held a great feast. Hagar and Ishmael were sent away.

ref: but the son of the slave woman is also your son, and I will make his descendants into a great nation.”
text: Na afenaa ba no nso, mɛyɛ no ɔman kɛseɛ, ɛfiri sɛ ɔyɛ w’aseni.
translated: And as for the son of the slave woman, I will make him into a great nation, because he is your offspring.

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.
text: Na Onyankopɔn buee n’ani, na ɔhunuu subura bi, na ɔkɔ kɔsaa bi hyɛɛ nsuo kotokuo no ma, na ɔde bi maa abarimaa no nomee.
translated: And God opened his eyes, and he saw a spring, and he went and filled the water bottle, and gave the boy to drink.

ref: Abraham separated seven female lambs from his flock of sheep,
text: Na Abraham yii nnwanten mma abereɛ nson gyinaa fa.
translated: And Abraham set aside seven ewe lambs as a witness.

ref: So they called the place Beersheba, because they made a treaty there.
text: Enti ɔfrɛɛ hɔ Beer-seba, ɛfiri sɛ ɛhɔ na wɔn baanu nyinaa kekaa ntam.
translated: So he called that place Beersheba, because it was there that the two of them swore an oath.

ref: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.
text: Na ɔsomfoɔ no de ne nsa kaa ne wura Abraham asene mu, na ɔkaa no ntam wɔ asɛm no ho.
translated: And the servant put his hand under his master Abraham's thigh, and he swore to him concerning the matter.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Na ɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ sɛ: AWURADE, me wura Abraham Nyankopɔn, mesrɛ wo, ma no nsi me yie nnɛ, na yɛ me wura Abraham adɔeɛ.
translated: And he prayed saying: LORD, God of my master Abraham, I beg you, let this be successful for me today, and show kindness to my master Abraham.

ref: while I am here at the well. When a young woman comes out to get water, I'll ask her to give me a drink.
text: hwɛ, megyina asubura yi ho, na ma no mmra sɛ ababaa a ɔbɛfiri abɛsa nsuo no, na mɛse no sɛ: Mesrɛ wo, ma me w’ahina yi mu nsuo kakra mennom,
translated: Look, I am standing by this river, and I will wait for the girl who comes to fetch water, and I will say to her: Please, give me a little water from your jar to drink,

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: na mebɔɔ me mu ase mesɔree AWURADE, na mehyiraa AWURADE, me wura Abraham Nyankopɔn a ɔkyerɛɛ me nokorɛ kwan sɛ menkɔware me wura nua ba memma ne ba no.
translated: So I bowed down and worshipped the LORD, and I blessed the LORD, the God of my master Abraham, who had led me on the right way to take the daughter of my master's brother for his son.

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: Na ɔne nnipa a wɔka ne ho no didii na wɔnomeeɛ no, wɔdaa hɔ anadwo no. Na wɔsɔree anɔpa no, ɔkaa sɛ: Monnya me kwan, na menkɔ me wura nkyɛn.
translated: He ate and drank with the people who were with him, and they spent the night there. And when they got up in the morning, he said: Allow me to go, so I may return to my master.

ref: and Isaac was so successful that he became very rich.
text: Na Isak nyaa ne ho, na ɔkɔɔ so ara nyaa ne ho kɔsii sɛ ɔyɛɛ kɛseɛ pa ara.
translated: And Isaac became prosperous, and he continued to prosper until he became very great.

ref: So Jacob brought the meat to his mother, and she cooked the tasty food that his father liked.
text: Na ɔkɔkyeree wɔn brɛɛ ne na, na ne na de yɛɛ akɔnnɔduane sɛdeɛ n’agya pɛ.
translated: He fetched water for his mother, and his mother used it to prepare a favorite meal as his father liked.

ref: Isaac asked, “Are you really my son Esau?” “Yes, I am,” Jacob answered.
text: Na ɔbisaa no sɛ: Ampa ara sɛ wone me ba Esau anaa? Na ɔse: Mene no.
translated: Then he asked him, "Are you really my son Esau?" And he said, "I am."

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: Na ɔno nso bɛnoaa akɔnnɔduane de brɛɛ n’agya ka kyerɛɛ n’agya sɛ: M’agya sɔre ɛ, na di wo ba hanam, na wo kra nhyira me!
translated: And she also cooked a delicious meal for her father and said to him: My father, get up and eat your child's meat, and may your soul bless me!

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
text: Wo nkrantɛ so na wonam bɛtena aseɛ, na wobɛsom wo nua. Nanso ɛbɛba sɛ wobɔ mmɔden kentene wo ho a, wobɛyi ne kɔnnua afiri wo kɔn so. Esau repɛ Yakob aku no
translated: You will live by your sword and serve your brother. But when you become restless and struggle hard, you will break his yoke from your neck. Esau is seeking to kill Jacob.

ref: At sunset he stopped for the night and went to sleep, resting his head on a large rock.
text: Na ɔduruu baabi no ɔsoɛɛ hɔ anadwo no, ɛfiri sɛ na owia akɔtɔ. Na ɔfaa hɔ aboɔ no bi de yɛɛ sumiiɛ, na ɔdaa hɔ.
translated: And when he got there that night, it was because the sun had set. He picked up some of the stones there to make a pillow, and he slept there.

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Na Yakob sii mu kɔɔ nnipa a wɔte apueeɛ fam asase so.
translated: And Jacob continued on to the people who live in the eastern land.

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: Na ɔhunui asubura bi wɔ wiram, na hwɛ, nnwankuo mmiɛnsa boaboa ho wɔ hɔ, ɛfiri sɛ abura no mu nsuo na wɔsa ma nnwankuo no nom, na ɔboɔ kokuroo da abura no ano.
translated: And he saw a stream in the forest, and behold, three herds of sheep were gathered there, because the water from the stream was what the herds drank, and a large stone lay at the mouth of the stream.

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Na Laban bisaa Yakob sɛ: Ɛnam sɛ woyɛ me busuani nti, wobɛsom me kwa anaa? Kyerɛ me deɛ ɛnyɛ w’akatua.
translated: Then Laban asked Jacob: Since you are my relative, will you serve me for nothing? Tell me what your wages should be.

ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Na Yakob dɔ Rahel, na ɔkaa sɛ: Wo ba kumaa Rahel nti mɛsom wo mfeɛ nson.
translated: Now Jacob loved Rachel, so he said: For your younger daughter Rachel, I will serve you seven years.

ref: But Jacob became upset with Rachel and answered, “Don't blame me! I'm not God.”
text: Na Yakob bo fuu Rahel kaa sɛ: Mesi Nyankopɔn a ɔkame wo wo yam mma no ananmu anaa?
translated: Then Jacob became angry with Rachel and said, "Am I in the place of God, who has kept you from having children?"

ref: Leah exclaimed, “I'm happy now, and all the women will say how happy I am.” So she named him Asher. Love Flowers
text: Na Lea kaa sɛ: M’ahotɔ nie! Na mmaa bɛfrɛ me deɛ wɔahyira no. Na ɔtoo no din Aser.
translated: And Leah said, "This is my happiness! For women will call me blessed." So she named him Asher.

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: Na ɛno akyiri no, ɔwoo ɔbabaa, na ɔtoo no din Dina.
translated: And after that, she gave birth to a daughter, and she named her Dinah.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Fa me yerenom ne me mma a wɔn nti mesomm woɔ no ma me. Wo ara wonim me som a masom woɔ.
translated: Give me my wife and my children for whom I have served you. You yourself know the service I have rendered you.

ref: in front of the branches, and their young were spotted and speckled.
text: Na nnwan no nyenyem mmaa no so, na wɔwowoo nsensannsensan, ntokontrama ne nworonnworan.
translated: But the sheep did not give birth to females, instead they gave birth to spotted, speckled, and striped ones.

ref: When your father said the speckled sheep would be my wages, all of them were speckled. And when he said the spotted ones would be mine, all of them were spotted.
text: Sɛ ɔka sɛ: Wɔn a wɔn ho yɛ ntokontrama no nyɛ w’akatua a, na nnwan no nyinaa awowo ntokontrama. Na sɛ ɔka sɛ: Wɔn a wɔn ho yɛ nsensannsensan no nyɛ w’akatua a, na nnwan no nyinaa awowo nsensannsensan.
translated: If he says: Those that are spotted are not your wages, then all the sheep give birth to spotted ones. And if he says: Those that are speckled are not your wages, then all the sheep give birth to speckled ones.

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.
text: Na Laban kɔ rekɔtwitwa ne nnwan ho nwi. Na Rahel wiaa n’agya abusua honi.
translated: And Laban went to shear his sheep. And Rachel stole her father's household gods.

ref: Jacob had set up camp in the hill country of Gilead, when Laban and his relatives came and set up camp in another part of the hill country. Laban went to Jacob
text: Na Laban kɔtoo Yakob. Na Yakob asi ne ntomadan wɔ bepɔ no so, na Laban ne ne nkurɔfoɔ nso sii wɔn deɛ Gilead bepɔ so.
translated: Then Laban caught up with Jacob. Jacob had pitched his tent on the mountain, and Laban with his relatives camped on the hill of Gilead.

ref: Laban also said, “This pile of rocks means that the Lord will watch us both while we are apart from each other.” So the place was also named Mizpah.
text: ne Mispa, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Sɛ yɛtete mu a, AWURADE nhwɛ me ne wo ntam.
translated: at Mizpah, because he said: May the LORD watch between me and you when we are apart from each other.

ref: Early the next morning, Laban kissed his daughters and his grandchildren goodbye, then he left to go back home.
text: Na Laban sɔree anɔpatutu, na ɔfee ne nananom ne ne mmammaa no ano hyiraa wɔn, na ɔsii mu sane kɔɔ ne kurom.
translated: And Laban rose up early in the morning, and kissed his sons and his daughters, and blessed them, and he departed and returned unto his place.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Na Yakob tuu abɔfoɔ dii n’anim kɔɔ ne nua Esau nkyɛn Seir asase so wɔ Edom wiram.
translated: And Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to his brother Esau in the land of Seir, the country of Edom.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: Na ɔde hyɛɛ ne nkoa nsa akuakuo, na ɔka kyerɛɛ ne nkoa no sɛ: Monsene nni m’anim, na momma kuo ne kuo ntam ntwe.
translated: And he put his servants into groups, and he told his servants: Go on ahead of me, and let there be space between each group.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Na Yakob hunuu wɔn no, ɔkaa sɛ: Nyankopɔn asafo nie. Na ɔtoo hɔ din: Maha-naim.
translated: When Jacob saw them, he said, "This is God's camp." So he named that place Mahanaim.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Na Yakob kaa sɛ: M’agya Abraham Nyankopɔn ne m’agya Isak Nyankopɔn, AWURADE a wosee me sɛ: Sane kɔ w’asase so ne w’abusua mu, na mɛyɛ wo yie no,
translated: And Jacob said: The God of my father Abraham and the God of my father Isaac, the LORD who said to me, "Return to your own land and to your family, and I will do you good,"

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Na monka nsi so sɛ: Hwɛ, w’akoa Yakob wɔ yɛn akyi. Afei ɔkaa sɛ: Mede ayɛyɛdeɛ a ɛdi m’anim yi merekɔpata no, ansa na mahunu n’anim; ebia ɔbɛhwɛ m’anim.
translated: And say also: Behold, your servant Jacob is behind us. Then he said: I am going ahead to appease him with this gift that goes before me, before I see his face; perhaps he will accept me.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Yakob too ɛhɔ din Peni-el, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Me ne Onyankopɔn adi nhunuanimu, nso me kra anya nkwa.
translated: And Jacob named that place Peniel, because he said: I have seen God face to face, yet my life has been preserved.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Manya anantwie, mfunumu, nnwan, mmirekyie ne nkoa ne mfenaa; na masoma sɛ wɔmmɛka nkyerɛ me wura, sɛdeɛ mɛnya w’anim adom.
translated: I have acquired goats, sheep, rams, male and female servants; and I have sent them to inform my master, so that I may find favor in your sight.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Na Yakob kaa sɛ: M’agya Abraham Nyankopɔn ne m’agya Isak Nyankopɔn, AWURADE a wosee me sɛ: Sane kɔ w’asase so ne w’abusua mu, na mɛyɛ wo yie no,
translated: And Jacob said: The God of my father Abraham and the God of my father Isaac, the LORD who said to me, “Return to your land and to your family, and I will do you good,”

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Na ayɛyɛdeɛ no sene dii n’anim; na ɔno deɛ, ɔtenaa nsraban no mu anadwo no. Yakob ne ɔbɔfoɔ bi tentam
translated: And the gift went ahead of him; but he himself spent the night in the camp. Jacob wrestled with an angel.

ref: Why don't you start letting your families marry into our families and ours marry into yours?
text: Na mo ne yɛn nni ayewareɛ, na momfa mo mmammaa mma yɛn, na mo nso monware yɛn mmammaa.
translated: You do not marry us, and you do not give your daughters to us, and you also do not marry our daughters.

ref: You can share this land with us. Move freely about until you find the property you want; then buy it and settle down here.
text: Na mo ne yɛn ntena, na asase na ɛda mo anim yi, montena so nyɛ so adwuma nnya so adeɛ.
translated: And you and your descendants, the land before you, live on it, work on it, and gain from it.

ref: Ask anything, no matter how expensive. I'll do anything, just let me marry Dinah.”
text: Monnye me etire adeɛ dodoɔ ne akyɛdeɛ biara, na biribiara a mobɛse me no, mɛma; na momfa ababaa no mma me awareɛ.
translated: Do not take too much of my headgear and any gift, and whatever you will ask of me, I will give; but do not give the girl to me in marriage.

ref: While Jacob and his family were traveling through Canaan, God terrified the people in the towns so much that no one dared bother them.
text: Na wɔsii mu sɛ wɔrekorɔ no, Nyankopɔn ho hu baa nkuro a atwa wɔn ho ahyia no so, na wɔanti Yakob mma no.
translated: And it was decided that as they were going, God's fear came upon the surrounding towns, and they did not pursue the sons of Jacob.

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Benjamin Is Born
text: Na Yakob frɛɛ deɛ ɔne Onyankopɔn kasaeɛ hɔ Betel. Rahel wuo
translated: And Jacob called the place where he spoke with God Bethel. Rachel died.

ref: He did this because the land was too crowded and could not support him and his brother with their flocks and herds.
text: ɛfiri sɛ wɔn ahonyadeɛ dɔɔso sene deɛ wɔde tena faako, na asase a wɔyɛ so ahɔhoɔ no, wɔn mmoa dodoɔ nti na wɔnsene so.
translated: Because their wealth is greater than what they use to live there, and since they are strangers on the land, it is because of the abundance of their animals that they do not move on.

ref: Shobal's sons were Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
text: Na yeinom ne Sobal mma: Alwan, Manahat, Ebal, Sefo ne Onam.
translated: And these are the sons of Sobal: Alwan, Manahat, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.

ref: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan, and they lived in the land of Seir.
text: otitire Dison, otitire Eser, otitire Disan. Yeinom ne Horifoɔ atitire sɛdeɛ wɔn mmusua teɛ wɔ Seir asase soɔ. Edom Ahemfo (
translated: Chief Dishon, Chief Ezer, Chief Dishan. These are the chiefs of the Horites, according to their clans in the land of Seir. Edomite Kings (

ref: Jacob lived in the land of Canaan, where his father Isaac had lived,
text: Na Yakob tenaa asase a n’agya yɛɛ so hɔhoɔ a ɛne Kanaan asase no so.
translated: And Jacob lived in the land where his father had stayed, the land of Canaan.

ref: which showed that Joseph was his favorite son, and so Joseph's brothers hated him and would not be friendly to him.
text: Na ne nuanom hunuu sɛ wɔn agya dɔ no sene wɔn nyinaa no, wɔtanee no, na wɔmfa abodwoɔ ne no nkasa.
translated: And when his brothers saw that their father loved him more than all of them, they hated him and could not speak peaceably to him.

ref: Reuben heard this and tried to protect Joseph from them. “Let's not kill him,” he said.
text: Na Ruben teeɛ no, ɔgyee no firii wɔn nsam kaa sɛ: Mommma yɛnnku no.
translated: When Reuben heard it, he rescued him from their hands, saying, "Let us not kill him."

ref: When Reuben returned to the well and did not find Joseph there, he tore his clothes in sorrow.
text: Na Ruben sane baa abura no so, na hwɛ, na Yosef nni abura no mu. Na ɔsunsuanee ne ntadeɛ mu,
translated: And Ruben returned to the pit, and behold, Joseph was not in the pit. And he tore his clothes,

ref: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
text: Na ɔrewoɔ no, ɔbaako yii ne nsa, na awogyefoɔ no sɔɔ mu de asaawa kɔkɔɔ kyekyeree ne nsa no kaa sɛ: Oyi na ɔfirii adi kane.
translated: And during the delivery, one child stretched out his hand, and the midwife took a red thread and tied it around his hand and said: This one came out first.

ref: and Potiphar's wife soon noticed him. She asked him to make love to her,
text: Na ɛbaa sɛ akyire yi no, Yosef wura yere tuu n’ani sii ne so, na ɔkaa sɛ: Wo ne me mmɛda.
translated: And it happened that later, his master's wife cast her eyes upon him, and she said: Lie with me.

ref: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
text: Na Yosef wura no faa no de no kɔtoo afiase, deɛ ɔhene nneduafoɔ guo, na ɔdaa afiase hɔ. Onyankopɔn di Yosef akyi
translated: So Joseph’s master took him and put him in prison, the place where the king’s prisoners were confined, and he was there in the prison. But God was with Joseph.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Nanso sɛ ɛyɛ a, kae me, na sɛ ɛsi wo yie a, yɛ me adɔeɛ, na bɔ me din kyerɛ Farao, na bɛyi me ɛdan yi mu,
translated: But if it happens, remember me, and if it goes well with you, do me a favor, and mention me to Pharaoh, and get me out of this house,

ref: and in three days the king will cut off your head. He will hang your body on a pole, and birds will come and peck at it.
text: na aka nnansa na Farao ayi wo afiri ha, na wakɔsɛne wo dua so ma nnomaa adi wo nam afiri wo ho.
translated: And within three days, Pharaoh will lift you from here, and will hang you on a tree so that birds will eat your flesh from you.

ref: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
text: Na paanootofoɔ panin no deɛ, ɔsɛnee no, sɛdeɛ Yosef kyerɛɛ wɔn aseɛ no.
translated: But the chief steward did as Joseph had instructed them.

ref: The king went back to sleep and had another dream. This time seven full heads of grain were growing on a single stalk.
text: Na ɔsane soo daeɛ bio, na hwɛ, ayuo mmɛtem nson a ɛtoatoa soɔ na abɔ ma refifiri wɔ nan korɔ so.
translated: And he dreamed again, and look, seven thin ears of grain, scorched by the east wind, were sprouting up on one stalk.

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
text: Na Onyankopɔn hunuu hann no sɛ ɛyɛ. Na Onyankopɔn paee hann ne esum no mu.
translated: And God saw that the light was good. And God separated the light from the darkness.

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Na Onyankopɔn frɛɛ ewiem no ɔsoro. Na adeɛ sae, na adeɛ kyee, da a ɛtɔ so mmienu ne no.
translated: And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: Na adeɛ sae, na adeɛ kyee, da a ɛtɔ so mmiɛnsa ne no.
translated: And night fell, and morning came, the third day.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Onyankopɔn bɔɔ onipa wɔ ne suban so. Aane, Nyankopɔn suban so na ɔbɔɔ no, ɔbarima ne ɔbaa na ɔbɔɔ wɔn.
translated: And God created man in His own image. Yes, in the image of God He created him; male and female He created them.

ref: I have provided all kinds of fruit and grain for you to eat.
text: Na Onyankopɔn kaa sɛ: Hwɛ, mede mfudeɛ a ɛwɔ asase ani nyinaa ne nnuaba nyinaa mama mo sɛ ɛnyɛ mo aduane.
translated: And God said: Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree with fruit yielding seed; to you it shall be for food.

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Na wɔwiee ɔsoro ne asase ne deɛ ɛwɔ mu nyinaa bɔ.
translated: And they finished creating the heavens and the earth and all that is in them.

ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: Na afuo mu nnua nyinaa nnya mmaa asase so, na afuo mu nhahan nyinaa nnya mfifirii, ɛfiri sɛ na AWURADE Nyankopɔn nnya ntɔɔ nsuo nguu asase no so, na na onipa a ɔbɛdɔ asase nni hɔ.
translated: And no shrub of the field had yet appeared on the earth and no plant of the field had yet sprung up, for the LORD God had not sent rain on the earth and there was no man to work the ground.

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: Na mmom papa ne bɔne ho nimdeɛ dua no deɛ, nni; ɛfiri sɛ da a wobɛdi bie no, wuo na wobɛwuo.
translated: But as for the knowledge of good and evil, do not eat it; because the day you eat it, you will surely die.

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,
text: Na AWURADE Nyankopɔn maa nnahɔɔ tɔɔ onipa no so, na ɔdae. Na ɔyii ne mfe mpadeɛ mu baako, na ɔde nam tuaa ananmu.
translated: And the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and he slept. And He took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh instead thereof.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Na onipa no kaa sɛ: Ampa ara, me nnompe mu dompe, ne me honam mu nam ankasa nie. Oyi na wɔbɛfrɛ no Ɔbaa, ɛfiri sɛ Ɔbarima mu na wɔyii no firiiɛ.
translated: And the person said: Truly, this is bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh. She shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.

ref: At once they saw what they had done, and they realized they were naked. Then they sewed fig leaves together to cover themselves.
text: Na wɔn baanu ani buebuee, na wɔhunuu sɛ wɔdeda adagya, enti wɔpempam borɔdɔma ahahan yɛɛ nkatanim fae.
translated: And the eyes of the two of them were opened, and they realized that they were naked, so they quickly sewed fig leaves together and made coverings for themselves.

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: Na mede ɔtan bɛto wo ne ɔbaa no ntam, ɛne w’asefoɔ ne n’asefoɔ ntam; ɔno na ɔbɛbɔ wo tire, na wo nso woaka ne nantin.
translated: And I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your offspring and hers; he will crush your head, and you will strike his heel.

ref: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
text: Na Adam deɛ, ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ: Sɛ woatie wo yere nne, na woadi dua a mehyɛɛ wo sɛ nnie no bie nti, wo nti wɔadome asase. Yea mu na wobɛdidi soɔ wo nkwa nna nyinaa.
translated: And Adam, he said to him: Because you listened to your wife’s voice and ate from the tree about which I commanded you not to eat, the ground is cursed because of you. Through painful toil you will eat from it all the days of your life.

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Na AWURADE Nyankopɔn yɛɛ nwoma ntadeɛ maa Adam ne ne yere de hyɛɛ wɔn. Wɔpamo Adam ne Hawa firi turo no mu
translated: And the LORD God made garments of clothing for Adam and his wife and clothed them. They drove Adam and Eve out of the garden.

ref: So the Lord God sent them out of the Garden of Eden, where they would have to work the ground from which the man had been made.
text: AWURADE Nyankopɔn tuu no firii Eden turom hɔ sɛ ɔnkɔdɔ asase a wɔfaa no firii mu no.
translated: The LORD God drove him out of the Garden of Eden to work the ground from which he had been taken.

ref: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?
text: Na AWURADE bisaa Kain sɛ: Adɛn nti na wo bo afuo na woamuna yi?
translated: And the LORD asked Cain: Why are you angry and why has your face fallen?

ref: If you had done the right thing, you would be smiling. But you did the wrong thing, and now sin is waiting to attack you like a lion. Sin wants to destroy you, but don't let it!
text: Sɛ woyɛɛ no yie a, anka w’anim rente anaa? Na sɛ woanyɛ no yie enti, bɔne butu ɛpono ano, na w’akyiri kwan na ɛhwehwɛ, nanso di ne so.
translated: If you had done it well, wouldn’t you have been accepted? But because you didn’t do it well, sin is crouching at the door, and it desires you, but you must rule over it.

ref: But Cain had to go far from the Lord and live in the Land of Wandering, which is east of Eden. More and More People
text: Na Kain firii AWURADE anim kɔe, na ɔkɔtenaa Nod asase so wɔ Eden apueeɛ. Kain asefoɔ
translated: So Cain went out from the presence of the LORD and lived in the land of Nod, east of Eden. The descendants of Cain

ref: One day, Lamech said to his two wives, “A young man wounded me, and I killed him.
text: Na Lamek ka kyerɛɛ ne yerenom sɛ: Ada ne Sila, montie me nne; Lamek yerenom, monyɛ aso mma m’asɛm: Mekumm ɔbarima sɛ ɔpiraa me, mekumm aberanteɛ sɛ ɔboroo me.
translated: And Lamech said to his wives: Adah and Zillah, listen to my voice; wives of Lamech, pay attention to my words: I have killed a man for wounding me, a young man for injuring me.

ref: Anyone who tries to get even with me will be punished ten times more than anyone who tries to get even with Cain.”
text: Na sɛ wɔtɔɔ Kain so werɛ mprɛnson a, Lamek deɛ bɛyɛ aduɔson nson. Set ne Enos awoɔ
translated: And if Cain was avenged seven times, then for Lamech it will be seventy-seven times. Seth and Enosh were born.

ref: Adam and his wife had another son. They named him Seth, because they said, “God has given us a son to take the place of Abel, who was killed by his brother Cain.”
text: Na Adam ne ne yere daeɛ bio, na ɔwoo ɔbabarima, na ɔtoo ne din Set sɛ: Onyankopɔn de ɔba foforɔ asi me Habel a Kain kumm no no ananmu.
translated: Adam slept with his wife again, and she gave birth to a son and named him Seth, saying: God has given me another child in place of Abel, whom Cain killed.

ref: More and more people were born, until finally they spread all over the earth. Some of their daughters were so beautiful that supernatural beings came down and married the ones they wanted.
text: Nyankopɔn mma hunuu nnipa mmammaa sɛ wɔn ho yɛ fɛ, na wɔwarewaree wɔn mu biara a wɔpɛ.
translated: God's children saw that humans were beautiful, and they married any of them they liked.

ref: He was sorry that he had made them,
text: Na AWURADE nuu ne ho sɛ ɔyɛɛ onipa wɔ asase soɔ, na ɛyɛɛ no ya n’akomam.
translated: And the LORD regretted that he had made man on the earth, and it grieved him at his heart.

ref: and he said, “I'm going to destroy every person on earth! I'll even wipe out animals, birds, and reptiles. I'm sorry I ever made them.”
text: Na AWURADE kaa sɛ: Mɛpepa nnipa a mabɔ wɔn no afiri asase ani, ampa ara, ɛfiri onipa so kɔsi ntɔteboa ne mmoa a wɔwea ne ewiem nnomaa so, ɛfiri sɛ manu me ho sɛ meyɛɛ wɔn.
translated: And the LORD said: I will wipe mankind, whom I have created, from the face of the earth—both people and animals, creatures that move along the ground and birds of the sky—for I regret that I have made them.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Na me ne wo bɛyɛ apam ato hɔ, na woakɔ adaka no mu, wo ne wo mma, ne wo yere, ne wo mma yerenom.
translated: And I will make a covenant with you, and you will enter the ark—you and your sons and your wife and your sons’ wives with you.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Na wo deɛ, boaboa aduane nyinaa bi ano kɔ mu, na ayɛ wo ne wɔn aduane.
translated: But as for you, you gather all kinds of food and keep it, and it becomes food for you and them.

ref: Also take seven pairs of every kind of bird with you. Do this so there will always be animals and birds on the earth.
text: ewiem nnomaa mu nso fa nson nson, anini ne abedeɛ, na wɔn ase nnya nkwa asase nyinaa ani.
translated: Among the birds of the sky, take seven each, male and female, so that their kind may survive throughout the earth.

ref: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
text: Na nsuo no yirii wɔ asase so nna ɔha aduonum.
translated: And the water dried upon the earth after one hundred and fifty days.

ref: Let out the birds, animals, and reptiles, so they can mate and live all over the earth.”
text: mmoadoma a wɔka wo ho nyinaa a ɛfiri honam nyinaa mu, nnomaa ne ntɔteboa ne deɛ ɛwea asase soɔ nyinaa ne wo mfiri adi, na wɔnwo, na wɔnnɔɔso asase so.
translated: All the creatures that are associated with you, from all over the body, birds and reptiles and all that move on the earth, they come from you, and they are born, and they multiply on the earth.

ref: After Noah and his family had left the boat,
text: Na Noa ne ne yere, ne ne mma ne wɔn yerenom firii adi;
translated: Then Noah and his wife, his sons and their wives came out.

ref: As long as the earth remains, there will be planting and harvest, cold and heat; winter and summer, day and night.
text: Nna a asase wɔ hɔ yi nyinaa, oguo ne otwa, awɔ ne ahuhuro, asuso ne ɔpɛ, awia ne anadwo rennyae da.
translated: As long as the earth exists, planting and harvesting, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night will never cease.

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.
text: Me nyankontɔn na mede masi mununkum mu, na ayɛ me ne asase ntam apam no ho nsɛnkyerɛnneɛ.
translated: My rainbow that I have set in the clouds is a sign of the covenant between me and the earth.

ref: When I see the rainbow in the sky, I will always remember the promise that I have made to every living creature.
text: Na nyankontɔn no bɛba mununkum mu, na mahwɛ, na mede makae daa apam a ɛwɔ Onyankopɔn ne nnipa ne abɔdeɛ mu ateasefoɔ a ɛwɔ asase soɔ nyinaa no ntam.
translated: And the rainbow will appear in the clouds, and I will look at it and remember the everlasting covenant between God and humans and every living creature of all flesh that is on the earth.

ref: All people on earth are descendants of Noah's three sons.
text: Yeinom ne Noa mma baasa no. Wɔn mu na nnipa firi petee asase nyinaa soɔ.
translated: These are the three sons of Noah. It is through them that people spread across the whole earth.

ref: One day he got drunk and was lying naked in his tent.
text: Na ɔnom nsa no bi, na ɔboroe, na ɔda ne ntomadan mu a ne ho da hɔ.
translated: And he drank some alcohol, and he became drunk, and he lay uncovered inside his tent.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Na Noa ani so tete firii ne nsaborɔ mu na ɔtee deɛ ne ba kumaa no ayɛ no no,
translated: Noah had been worried for a long time, and when he heard what had happened to his little child,

ref: he said, “I now put a curse on Canaan! He will be the lowest slave of his brothers.
text: ɔkaa sɛ: Wɔadome Kanaan, ɔbɛyɛ nkoa mu akoa ama ne nuanom.
translated: He said: When they have conquered Canaan, he will be a servant of servants to his brothers.

ref: But when the Lord came down to look at the city and the tower,
text: Na AWURADE siane bɛhwɛɛ kuro ne abantenten a nnipa mma retoɔ no.
translated: And the LORD looked down upon the city and the tower which the sons of men were building.

ref: When the Egyptians see you, they will murder me because I am your husband. But they won't kill you.
text: na sɛ Misraimfoɔ hu wo a, wɔbɛka sɛ: Ne yere nie, na wɔaku me, na wɔama woatena ase.
translated: And when the Egyptians see you, they will say, “This is his wife,” and they will kill me but let you live.

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Adɛn nti na wose: Ɔyɛ me nua? Anka meremfa no nware no; na afei, hwɛ wo yere nie, fa no na kɔ wo kwan.
translated: Why then do you say: He is my brother? Otherwise, I wouldn't have taken him in as a husband; and now, look, here is your wife, take her and go your way.

ref: Abram stayed in the land of Canaan. But Lot settled near the cities of the valley and put up his tents not far from Sodom,
text: Abram tenaa Kanaan asase so, na Lot deɛ, ɔtenaa Yordan kanko no nkuro mu, na ɔtutuu ne ntomadan ara de kɔduruu Sodom.
translated: Abram lived in the land of Canaan, but Lot lived among the towns of the Jordan plain and pitched his tents near Sodom.

ref: After Abram and Lot had gone their separate ways, the Lord said to Abram: Look around to the north, south, east, and west.
text: Na Lot tee ne ho firii Abram ho no, AWURADE ka kyerɛɛ Abram sɛ: Ma w’ani so, na firi deɛ wowɔ hɔ hwɛ atifi fam ne anafoɔ fam, apueeɛ ne atɔeɛ.
translated: When Lot had separated from Abram, the LORD said to Abram: Lift up your eyes from where you are and look north and south, east and west.

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: Na asase a wohunu yi nyinaa, wo ne w’asefoɔ na mede bɛma mo daa.
translated: And all this land that you see, I will give to you and your descendants forever.

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Na Abram tutuu ne ntomadan, na ɔbɛtenaa Mamre pɔ a ɛwɔ Hebron no ho, na ɔsii afɔrebukyia maa AWURADE wɔ hɔ.
translated: Then Abram moved his tent and went to live near the great trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar to the LORD.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Na afe a ɛtɔ so dunan mu no, Kedorlaomer ne ahemfo a wɔkeka ne ho no bɛkum Refaim wɔ Asterot-karnaim, ne Susifoɔ wɔ Ham, ne Emifoɔ Kiriataim mpaperɛ so,
translated: And in the fourteenth year, Kedorlaomer and the kings who were with him defeated the Rephaim in Ashteroth Karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, and the Emim in the plain of Kiriathaim,

ref: They also defeated the Horites in the hill country of Edom, as far as El-Paran, near the desert.
text: ne Horifoɔ wɔ wɔn bepɔ Seir so de kɔsii El-paran a ɛwɔ ɛserɛ soɔ no.
translated: And his allies were in the hill country of Seir until El-paran, which is in the wilderness.

ref: They went back to the city of Enmishpat, better known as Kadesh. Then they captured all the land that belonged to the Amalekites, and they defeated the Amorites who were living in Hazazon-Tamar.
text: Na wɔsane baa Enmispat, , na wɔkaa Amalekfoɔ asase nyinaa ne Amorifoɔ nso a wɔte Haseson-tamar no gui.
translated: And they returned to En Mishpat, and they destroyed all the land of the Amalekites and also the Amorites who lived in Hazezon-tamar.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Na wɔfaa Sodom ne Gomora nnoɔma ne wɔn nnuane nyinaa de sii mu kɔe.
translated: And they took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah and all their food and went away.

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek
text: Na ɔde nnoɔma no nyinaa ne ne nua ba Lot ne ne nnoɔma ne mmaa no nso ne nkurɔfoɔ no sane bae. Melkisedek hyira Abram
translated: And he brought back all the possessions, and also brought back his nephew Lot and his possessions, as well as the women and the people. Melchizedek blessed Abram.

ref: Abram returned after he had defeated King Chedorlaomer and the other kings. Then the king of Sodom went to meet Abram in Shaveh Valley, which is also known as King's Valley.
text: Na ɔsane firii Kedorlaomer ne ahemfo a ɛkeka ne ho so nkonimdie reba no, Sodom hene firi kɔhyiaa no wɔ Sawe bonhwa mu.
translated: And as he was returning from defeating Kedorlaomer and the kings allied with him, the king of Sodom went out to meet him in the Valley of Shaveh.

ref: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “All I want are my people. You can keep everything else.”
text: Na Sodom hene ka kyerɛɛ Abram sɛ: Fa nnipa no ma me, na fa ahodeɛ no.
translated: Then the king of Sodom said to Abram: Give me the people, and take the possessions for yourself.

ref: Then the Lord told him, “Bring me a three-year-old cow, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.”
text: Na AWURADE ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ: Fa nantwie ba a wadi mfeɛ mmiɛnsa, abirekyie a wadi mfeɛ mmiɛnsa, odwennini a wadi mfeɛ mmiɛnsa, anyankuku ne aborɔnoma ba brɛ me.
translated: And the LORD said to him: Bring a three-year-old heifer, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a turtledove, and a young pigeon to me.

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Na nnomaa a wɔdi nam bɛsisii mmoa afunu no so, na Abram pamm wɔn.
translated: And the birds that ate meat came down on the carcasses, but Abram drove them away.

ref: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaites,
text: Hetifoɔ, Perisifoɔ, Refaimfoɔ,
translated: Hittites, Perizzites, Rephaites,

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: Na ɔbɛyɛ onipa a ɔte sɛ ɛserɛ so afunumu. Ne nsa bɛtia nnipa nyinaa, na nnipa nyinaa nsa atia no, na ɔbɛsɔre atia ne nuanom nyinaa.
translated: And he will be a person as wild as a donkey. His hand will be against everyone, and everyone's hand will be against him, and he will rise up against all his brothers.

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Na ɔfrɛɛ AWURADE a ɔne no kasaeɛ no din sɛ: Wone El-roi, na ɔse: Mahunu deɛ ɔhu me no ampa anaa?
translated: And she called the name of the LORD who spoke to her: You are El-roi, for she said, Have I truly seen the One who sees me?

ref: That's why people call the well between Kadesh and Bered, “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Enti wɔfrɛ asubura no sɛ: Beer-lahai-roi, na hwɛ, ɛwɔ Kades ne Bared ntam.
translated: So the well was called Beer-lahai-roi; it is between Kadesh and Bered.

ref: Abram bowed with his face to the ground, and God said:
text: Na Abram daa hɔ de n’anim butuu fam; na Onyankopɔn ne no kasaa sɛ:
translated: Abram stayed there with his face to the ground; and God spoke with him saying:

ref: I promise that you will be the father of many nations. So now I'm changing your name from Abram to Abraham.
text: Na wɔremfrɛ wo din Abram bio, na mmom wɔbɛfrɛ wo Abraham, ɛfiri sɛ mayɛ wo aman pii agya.
translated: And your name will no longer be called Abram, but rather you will be called Abraham, because I have made you a father of many nations.

ref: I will always keep the promise I have made to you and your descendants, because I am your God and their God.
text: Na mɛma me ne wo ne w’asefoɔ a ɛbɛdi w’akyire no apam agyina wɔ wɔn awoɔ ntoatoasoɔ mu, na ayɛ daa apam, na mayɛ wo ne w’asefoɔ a ɛbɛdi w’akyire no Nyankopɔn.
translated: And I will establish my covenant with you and your descendants after you throughout their generations, as an everlasting covenant, and I will be God to you and to your descendants after you.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Na Onyankopɔn ka kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ: Wo yere Sarai deɛ, mfrɛ no Sarai bio, na mmom Sara.
translated: And God said to Abraham: As for your wife Sarai, do not call her Sarai anymore, but rather Sarah.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Na Onyankopɔn kaa sɛ: Ampaneampa, wo yere Sara bɛwo ɔba ama wo, na woato ne din Isak. Na mɛma me ne no apam no agyina ayɛ daa apam ama n’asefoɔ a ɛbɛdi n’akyire no.
translated: And God said: Truly, your wife Sarah will bear a son for you, and you shall name him Isaac. And I will establish my covenant with him as an everlasting covenant for his descendants after him.

ref: He then served his guests some yogurt and milk together with the meat. While they were eating, he stood near them under the tree,
text: Na Abraham faa nufosuo ani sradeɛ ne nufosuo ne nantwie ba nam a ɔsiesiee no no, na ɔde bɛsii wɔn anim; na ɔno deɛ, ɔgyinaa wɔn nkyɛn dua no ase, maa wɔdidii.
translated: And Abraham took butter and milk and the calf which he had prepared, and set it before them; and he stood by them under the tree, and they ate.

ref: The Lord asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh? Does she doubt that she can have a child in her old age?
text: Na AWURADE bisaa Abraham sɛ: Adɛn nti na Sara seree sɛ: Ampaneampa ara manyini yi mɛwo ɔba?
translated: And the LORD asked Abraham: Why did Sarah laugh, saying: Truly, will I bear a child now that I am old?

ref: Then he said, “Friends, please don't do such a terrible thing!
text: Na ɔkaa sɛ: Anuanom, mesrɛ mo, monnyɛ bɔne saa!
translated: And he said: Brothers, I beg you, do not do this evil!

ref: I have two daughters who have never had sex. I'll bring them out, and you can do what you want with them. But don't harm these men. They are guests in my home.”
text: Hwɛ, mewɔ mmammaa baanu a wɔnhunuu ɔbarima, momma memfa wɔn mfiri mmrɛ mo, na monyɛ wɔn sɛdeɛ ɛyɛ mo ani soɔ na mmarima yi nko deɛ, monnyɛ wɔn hwee, na ɛno nti na wɔbaa me suhyɛ aseɛ yi.
translated: Look, I have two daughters who have not known a man; let me bring them out to you, and do to them as you please, but to these men, do nothing, for that is why they have come under the protection of my roof.

ref: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
text: Hwɛ, kuro yi na ɛbɛn sɛ wɔdwane kɔ hɔ, ɛhɔ sua. Ma mennwane nkɔ hɔ ɛ! Ɛnsua anaa? Na me kra anya nkwa.
translated: Look, this town is nearby, let me run there; it is small. Let me run there! Is it not small? And my soul will live.

ref: Let's get our father drunk! Then we can sleep with him and have children.”
text: Bra, na yɛmma yɛn agya nsa nnom, na yɛne no nkɔda, na yɛanya yɛn agya ho mma.
translated: Come, let us give our father something to drink, and let us sleep with him, so that we may preserve our father's lineage.

ref: That's how Lot's two daughters had children.
text: Na Lot mma maa baanu no nyinaa nyinsɛn wɔn agya ho.
translated: So both of Lot's daughters became pregnant by their father.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Na Onyankopɔn baa Abimelek nkyɛn anadwo daeɛ mu, na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ: Hwɛ, ɔbaa a woakɔfa no no nti, woawu awie, ɛfiri sɛ ɔyɛ ɔbarima bi yere.
translated: But God came to Abimelech in a dream at night and said to him, "Behold, you are as good as dead because of the woman you have taken; she is a man's wife."

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
text: Na Abimelek kaa sɛ: Hwɛ, m’asase na ɛda w’anim yi, baabiara a ɛyɛ wɔ w’ani soɔ no, tena.
translated: And Abimelech said: Look, my land is before you; settle wherever is pleasing in your sight.

ref: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.
text: Na Abraham ba Isak dii nnawɔtwe no, ɔtwaa no twetia sɛdeɛ Onyankopɔn hyɛɛ no no.
translated: When Abraham's son Isaac was eight days old, he circumcised him as God had commanded him.

ref: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away
text: Na abɔfra no nyinii, na wɔtwaa no nufoɔ. Na da a wɔtwaa Isak nufoɔ no, Abraham too pono kɛseɛ. Wɔpamo Hagar ne Ismael
translated: And the child grew, and was weaned. And on the day Isaac was weaned, Abraham held a great feast. Hagar and Ishmael were sent away.

ref: but the son of the slave woman is also your son, and I will make his descendants into a great nation.”
text: Na afenaa ba no nso, mɛyɛ no ɔman kɛseɛ, ɛfiri sɛ ɔyɛ w’aseni.
translated: And as for the son of the slave woman, I will make him a great nation, because he is your offspring.

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.
text: Na Onyankopɔn buee n’ani, na ɔhunuu subura bi, na ɔkɔ kɔsaa bi hyɛɛ nsuo kotokuo no ma, na ɔde bi maa abarimaa no nomee.
translated: And God opened his eyes, and he saw a well of water, and he went and filled the water bottle, and gave the boy a drink.

ref: Abraham separated seven female lambs from his flock of sheep,
text: Na Abraham yii nnwanten mma abereɛ nson gyinaa fa.
translated: And Abraham selected seven ewe lambs and set them apart.

ref: Abraham replied, “I want you to accept these seven lambs as proof that I dug this well.”
text: Na ɔkaa sɛ: Nnwanten mma abereɛ nson yi, gye wɔ me nsam, na ɛnyɛ me adanseɛ sɛ me na metuu abura yi.
translated: And he said: Bring me these seven lambs, and let them be a witness that I dug this well.

ref: So they called the place Beersheba, because they made a treaty there.
text: Enti ɔfrɛɛ hɔ Beer-seba, ɛfiri sɛ ɛhɔ na wɔn baanu nyinaa kekaa ntam.
translated: So he called that place Beersheba, because it was there that the two of them swore an oath.

ref: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.
text: Na wɔpamee wɔ Beer-seba; na Abimelek ne ne safohene Pikol sɔree, na wɔsane kɔɔ Filistifoɔ asase so.
translated: And they made an agreement at Beersheba; then Abimelech and Phicol his army commander got up, and they returned to the land of the Philistines.

ref: The Lord said, “Go get Isaac, your only son, the one you dearly love! Take him to the land of Moriah, and I will show you a mountain where you must sacrifice him to me on the fires of an altar.”
text: Na ɔkaa sɛ: Fa wo ba, wo ba korɔ a wodɔ no, Isak, kɔ Moria asase so, na fa no kɔbɔ ɔhyeɛ afɔdeɛ wɔ hɔ, wɔ mmepɔ no mu baako a mɛkyerɛ woɔ so.
translated: And he said: Take your son, your only son whom you love, Isaac, go to the land of Moriah, and offer him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains I will show you.

ref: The Lord's angel called out from heaven a second time:
text: Na AWURADE bɔfoɔ firi soro frɛɛ Abraham bio sɛ:
translated: And the angel of the LORD called to Abraham from heaven again saying:

ref: You have obeyed me, and so you and your descendants will be a blessing to all nations on earth.”
text: Na w’asefoɔ mu na wɔbɛhyira asase so amanaman nyinaa, ɛfiri sɛ woatie me nne.
translated: And through your descendants all the nations of the earth will be blessed, because you have obeyed my voice.

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: Na ne mpena baako bi a wɔfrɛ no Rehuma nso woo Teba, Gaham, Tahas ne Maaka.
translated: And his concubine, whose name was Rehumah, also bore Teba, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.

ref: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.
text: Na ɔsomfoɔ no de ne nsa kaa ne wura Abraham asene mu, na ɔkaa no ntam wɔ asɛm no ho.
translated: And the servant put his hand under his master Abraham's thigh, and swore to him concerning the matter.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Na ɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ sɛ: AWURADE, me wura Abraham Nyankopɔn, mesrɛ wo, ma no nsi me yie nnɛ, na yɛ me wura Abraham adɔeɛ.
translated: And he prayed saying: LORD, God of my master Abraham, I beg you, let it go well with me today, and show kindness to my master Abraham.

ref: Abraham's servant did not say a word, but he watched everything Rebekah did, because he wanted to know for certain if this was the woman the Lord had chosen.
text: Na ɔbarima no hwɛɛ no ara, na ɔyɛɛ komm sɛ ɔbɛhunu sɛ AWURADE ama ne kwankorɔ no ayɛ dwoodwoo anaasɛ ɛnte saa.
translated: And the man looked at him closely and remained silent to see whether the LORD had made his journey successful or not.

ref: Rebekah ran straight home and told her family everything.
text: Na ababaa no tuu mmirika kɔkaa saa nsɛm yi kyerɛɛ ne na fiefoɔ.
translated: And the girl ran and told this to her mother’s family.

ref: while I am here at the well. When a young woman comes out to get water, I'll ask her to give me a drink.
text: hwɛ, megyina asubura yi ho, na ma no mmra sɛ ababaa a ɔbɛfiri abɛsa nsuo no, na mɛse no sɛ: Mesrɛ wo, ma me w’ahina yi mu nsuo kakra mennom,
translated: Look, I am standing by this stream, and when a young woman comes to fetch water, I will say to her: Please, give me a little water from your jar to drink,

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: na mebɔɔ me mu ase mesɔree AWURADE, na mehyiraa AWURADE, me wura Abraham Nyankopɔn a ɔkyerɛɛ me nokorɛ kwan sɛ menkɔware me wura nua ba memma ne ba no.
translated: So I bowed down and worshipped the LORD, and I blessed the LORD, the God of my master Abraham, who led me on the right path to take the daughter of my master's brother for his son.

ref: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
text: Na ɔsomfoɔ no yii dwetɛ nnwinneɛ ne sika kɔkɔɔ nnwinneɛ ne ntadeɛ de maa Rebeka; na ɔde nnoɔma a ɛsom boɔ maa ne nuabarima ne ne na.
translated: And the servant brought out silver ornaments and gold ornaments and clothing and gave them to Rebekah; and he also gave valuable things to her brother and her mother.

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: Na ɔne nnipa a wɔka ne ho no didii na wɔnomeeɛ no, wɔdaa hɔ anadwo no. Na wɔsɔree anɔpa no, ɔkaa sɛ: Monnya me kwan, na menkɔ me wura nkyɛn.
translated: And he ate and drank with the people who were with him, and they spent the night there. And when they got up in the morning, he said: Allow me to go, so I may return to my master.

ref: They answered, “Let's ask Rebekah what she wants to do.”
text: Na wɔse: Ma yɛmfrɛ ababaa no mmisa ɔno ara.
translated: And they said: Let us call the girl herself to ask her.

ref: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, “Who is that man?” “He is my master Isaac,” the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.
text: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ ɔsomfoɔ no sɛ: Ɔbarima bɛn na ɔnam wira mu rebɛhyia yɛn yi? Na ɔsomfoɔ no se: Ɛyɛ me wura. Na ɔfaa ne nkatanimu de kataa n’anim.
translated: And he said to the servant: Which man is that coming to meet us through the field? And the servant said: It is my master. Then she took her veil and covered her face.

ref: Once during Abraham's lifetime, the fields had not produced enough grain, and now the same thing happened. So Isaac went to King Abimelech of the Philistines in the land of Gerar,
text: Na ɛkɔm baa asase no so, ɛsono ɛkɔm a ɛbaa Abraham berɛ soɔ no. Na Isak kɔɔ Filistifoɔ hene Abimelek nkyɛn wɔ Gerar.
translated: Now there was famine in the land, a famine like the one that occurred during Abraham's time. So Isaac went to Abimelech, king of the Philistines, in Gerar.

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: Na ɛhɔfoɔ no bisaa no ne yere ho asɛm no, ɔkaa sɛ: Me nua ne no. Na ɔsuro sɛ ɔbɛka sɛ: Me yere ne no, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Anyɛ saa a, Rebeka nti ɛhafoɔ bɛku me; ɛfiri sɛ ne ho yɛ fɛ.
translated: And when the people there asked him about his wife, he said, "She is my sister." He was afraid to say, "She is my wife," because he thought, "If I say so, because of Rebekah, the people here will kill me; for she is beautiful."

ref: and Isaac was so successful that he became very rich.
text: Na Isak nyaa ne ho, na ɔkɔɔ so ara nyaa ne ho kɔsii sɛ ɔyɛɛ kɛseɛ pa ara.
translated: And Isaac became prosperous, and he continued to prosper until he became very great.

ref: While his servants were digging in the valley, they found a spring-fed well.
text: Na Isak nkoa funuu bonhwa no mu, na wɔhunuu nsupa abura wɔ hɔ.
translated: And Isaac's servants dug in the valley, and found there a well of springing water.

ref: she said to Jacob, “I heard your father tell Esau
text: Na Rebeka ka kyerɛɛ ne ba Yakob sɛ: Hwɛ, metee sɛ w’agya rekasa kyerɛ wo nua Esau sɛ:
translated: Now Rebekah said to her son Jacob: Look, I heard your father speaking to your brother Esau saying:

ref: “My brother Esau is a hairy man,” Jacob reminded her. “And I am not.
text: Na Yakob ka kyerɛɛ ne na Rebeka sɛ: Hwɛ, me nua Esau ho wɔ nwi, na me deɛ, me ho yɛ toromtorom.
translated: And Jacob said to his mother Rebekah: Look, my brother Esau is hairy, but as for me, my skin is smooth.

ref: So Jacob brought the meat to his mother, and she cooked the tasty food that his father liked.
text: Na ɔkɔkyeree wɔn brɛɛ ne na, na ne na de yɛɛ akɔnnɔduane sɛdeɛ n’agya pɛ.
translated: He fetched water for his mother, and his mother used it to prepare a favorite meal just as his father liked.

ref: She also covered the smooth part of his hands and neck with goatskins
text: Na ɔde mmirekyie mma no nwoma duraduraa ne nsa ne ne kɔn a ɛho yɛ toromtorom no ho.
translated: And he smeared the thick blood of the sheep on his hands and his neck, which was very dirty.

ref: Isaac asked, “Are you really my son Esau?” “Yes, I am,” Jacob answered.
text: Na ɔbisaa no sɛ: Ampa ara sɛ wone me ba Esau anaa? Na ɔse: Mene no.
translated: Then he asked him, "Are you really my son Esau?" And he said, "I am."

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: Na ɔno nso bɛnoaa akɔnnɔduane de brɛɛ n’agya ka kyerɛɛ n’agya sɛ: M’agya sɔre ɛ, na di wo ba hanam, na wo kra nhyira me!
translated: And she also prepared a delicious meal and brought it to her father, saying to him: My father, rise and eat your child's meat, so that your soul may bless me!

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Na n’agya Isak see no sɛ: Ɛyɛ wo hwan a? Na ɔse: Ɛyɛ me, wo ba, w’abakan Esau.
translated: And his father Isaac said to him, "Who are you?" And he said, "It is me, your son, your firstborn Esau."

ref: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
text: Na ɔse: Ne din nne Yakob anaa? Mprenu ni na watwintwane m’anan mu. Wafa me panin, na hwɛ, afei nso wagye me nhyira. Na ɔse: Woamfa nhyira bi ansie me?
translated: And he said: Is his name not Jacob? He has deceived me twice. He took my birthright, and now he has taken my blessing. And he said: Have you not reserved any blessing for me?

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
text: Wo nkrantɛ so na wonam bɛtena aseɛ, na wobɛsom wo nua. Nanso ɛbɛba sɛ wobɔ mmɔden kentene wo ho a, wobɛyi ne kɔnnua afiri wo kɔn so. Esau repɛ Yakob aku no
translated: You will live by your sword and serve your brother. But when you grow restless, you will throw his yoke from your neck. Esau is seeking to kill Jacob.

ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Na Rebeka ka kyerɛɛ Isak sɛ: Het mmammaa nti m’asetena afono me. Sɛ Yakob kɔware yerɛ Het mmammaa mu, sɛ yeinom a wɔwɔ ɛha yi a, na me mmerɛ ngui, na mete ase reyɛ deɛn bio?
translated: And Rebekah said to Isaac: Because of the Hittite women, my life is miserable. If Jacob marries a woman from among the Hittite women here, like these ones, what good will my life be to me?

ref: At sunset he stopped for the night and went to sleep, resting his head on a large rock.
text: Na ɔduruu baabi no ɔsoɛɛ hɔ anadwo no, ɛfiri sɛ na owia akɔtɔ. Na ɔfaa hɔ aboɔ no bi de yɛɛ sumiiɛ, na ɔdaa hɔ.
translated: And when he got there that night, because the sun had set, he took some of the stones there and used them as a pillow, and he slept there.

ref: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
text: Na ɔsuroe, na ɔse: Ɛha yi yɛ hu ampa. Ɛha nyɛ baabiara sɛ Nyankopɔn fie, na ɛha ne ɔsoro aboboano.
translated: And he was afraid, and said: This place is truly awesome. This is none other than the house of God, and this is the gate of heaven.

ref: When Jacob got up early the next morning, he took the rock that he had used for a pillow and stood it up as a place of worship. Then he poured olive oil on the rock to dedicate it to God,
text: Na Yakob sɔree anɔpatutu, na ɔfaa ɔboɔ a ɔde yɛɛ sumiiɛ no de yɛɛ odum sii hɔ, na ɔhwiee ngo guu so.
translated: And Jacob rose early in the morning, and he took the stone that he had used as a pillow and set it up as a pillar, and he poured oil on it.

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Na Yakob sii mu kɔɔ nnipa a wɔte apueeɛ fam asase so.
translated: And Jacob continued on to the people who live in the land of the east.

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: Na ɔhunui asubura bi wɔ wiram, na hwɛ, nnwankuo mmiɛnsa boaboa ho wɔ hɔ, ɛfiri sɛ abura no mu nsuo na wɔsa ma nnwankuo no nom, na ɔboɔ kokuroo da abura no ano.
translated: And he saw a stream in the forest, and behold, three herds of sheep were gathered there, because the water from the stream was being drawn for the flocks to drink, and a large stone lay at the mouth of the stream.

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Na wɔse: Yɛntumi, gye sɛ wɔaboaboa nnwankuo no nyinaa ano, na wɔamuni ɔboɔ no afiri abura no ano, ansa na yɛama nnwan no anom.
translated: And they said: We cannot, unless all the sheep are gathered together and the stone is rolled away from the mouth of the well, before we let the sheep drink.

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Na Laban bisaa Yakob sɛ: Ɛnam sɛ woyɛ me busuani nti, wobɛsom me kwa anaa? Kyerɛ me deɛ ɛnyɛ w’akatua.
translated: Then Laban asked Jacob: Because you are my relative, will you serve me for nothing? Tell me what your wages should be.

ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Na Yakob dɔ Rahel, na ɔkaa sɛ: Wo ba kumaa Rahel nti mɛsom wo mfeɛ nson.
translated: Now Jacob loved Rachel, so he said: For your younger daughter Rachel, I will serve you seven years.

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Na anadwofa no ɔde ne ba Lea brɛɛ no, na ɔne no dae.
translated: But in the evening he brought his daughter Leah to him, and he slept with her.

ref: But Jacob became upset with Rachel and answered, “Don't blame me! I'm not God.”
text: Na Yakob bo fuu Rahel kaa sɛ: Mesi Nyankopɔn a ɔkame wo wo yam mma no ananmu anaa?
translated: Then Jacob became angry with Rachel and said, "Am I in the place of God, who has kept you from having children?"

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Na Lea kaa sɛ: Aba yie. Na ɔtoo no din Gad.
translated: And Leah said, "Good fortune has come." So she named him Gad.

ref: Leah exclaimed, “I'm happy now, and all the women will say how happy I am.” So she named him Asher. Love Flowers
text: Na Lea kaa sɛ: M’ahotɔ nie! Na mmaa bɛfrɛ me deɛ wɔahyira no. Na ɔtoo no din Aser.
translated: And Lea said, "This is my happiness! For women will call me blessed." So she named him Asher.

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: Na ɛno akyiri no, ɔwoo ɔbabaa, na ɔtoo no din Dina.
translated: And after that, she gave birth to a daughter, and she named her Dinah.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Fa me yerenom ne me mma a wɔn nti mesomm woɔ no ma me. Wo ara wonim me som a masom woɔ.
translated: Give me my wife and my children for whom I have served you. You yourself know the service I have rendered you.

ref: Jacob answered: You've seen how hard I've worked for you, and you know how your flocks and herds have grown under my care.
text: Na Yakob see no sɛ: Wo ara wonim sɛdeɛ masom wo afa ne sɛdeɛ wo mmoakuo ayɛ wɔ m’aseɛ.
translated: And Jacob said to him: You yourself know how I have served you and how your livestock have fared with me.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: Mebaa wo nkyɛn no, na deɛ wowɔ no sua, na afei aseɛ atrɛ ayɛ pii, na AWURADE ahyira wo baabiara a mefa. Na afei da bɛn na menhwɛ me fie nso?
translated: I came to you when you had little, but now things have greatly increased, and the LORD has blessed you wherever I have been. So when will I also look to my own household?

ref: Let me go through your flocks and herds and take the sheep and goats that are either spotted or speckled and the black lambs. That's all you need to give me.
text: Ɛnnɛ mɛkɔ makɔfa wo nnwan nyinaa mu, na mayiyi emu nnwan a wɔn ho yɛ ntokontrama ne nworonnworan nyinaa ne nnwammire nyinaa wɔ nnwanten no mu ne mmirekyie no mu nworonnworan ne ntokontrama. Wɔn a wɔtete sɛɛ na ɛnyɛ m’akatua.
translated: Today I will go and bring all your sheep, and I will select from among them all the speckled and spotted sheep and all the black lambs among the sheep and the spotted and speckled among the goats. Such will be my wages.
